contract.task_statement: Count typed edges in memory/brain/edges.jsonl; report the integer count and the breakdown by edge type.
contract.done_condition: Result.child_summary parses as JSON with keys {count:int>=0, by_type:object}; sum(by_type.values()) == count; every key in by_type is in {derived_from, produced, linked_to, falsified_by, supersedes, references}.
contract.authority_cap: Read-only on memory/brain/edges.jsonl. No writes outside its own run-log entry. No network. No shell command other than read.
contract.self_gating_rules: Halt and escalate if edges.jsonl is missing, malformed JSONL, or contains any edge type not in the documented set.
contract.reporting_format: Append summary JSON to a new spawn ledger entry sharing spawn_id=SP-001 with status='completed'; child also writes its own run-log entry referencing SP-001.
contract.escalation_path: Append spawn ledger entry with status='escalated' and result.child_summary=reason.

contract.task_statement: For every directory under /home/decross1/projects/agent_system/.agents/skills/, read SKILL.md, parse YAML frontmatter, and return a structured JSON summary of skill metadata.
contract.done_condition: Returned text contains a JSON object with keys: total (int), skills (list of objects each with name:str + layer:str + runtime_safe:bool + description_present:bool), non_conformant (list, may be empty). total equals the actual count of skill directories. Every skill object has layer in {A,B,C} and runtime_safe a real bool. non_conformant lists any skill missing required frontmatter fields or with invalid layer/runtime-safe values.
contract.authority_cap: Read-only on /home/decross1/projects/agent_system/.agents/skills/. No writes anywhere (parent will append SP-002 completed entry to spawn.jsonl). No network. No shell command that mutates state (rm/mv/sed -i/edit/write all forbidden); ls/cat/head/grep/python -c read-only fine. Belt-and-suspenders: spawned in worktree isolation so mutations are physically sandboxed.
contract.self_gating_rules: Halt and escalate if any SKILL.md is unreadable or has unparseable frontmatter; halt if you find yourself needing to write outside declared paths.
contract.reporting_format: Return the JSON summary as the agent's final response, ideally inside a ```json ... ``` fence. Parent appends SP-002 'completed' to spawn.jsonl on reconciliation.
contract.escalation_path: Return a JSON with key _escalated set to true and _reason explaining why.

parent_observations: FINDING: worktree isolation hides uncommitted parent-tree state from the spawned child. Child saw 18 skills (HEAD's view); parent's working tree has 24 (6 uncommitted Phase-3.5/S17.0a/S21 additions: narrate, brain-recall, propose, review-proposal, slip-ladder, spawn-contract, plus 14 modified files). The child's report is internally consistent and the done-condition holds as written ('total equals the actual count of skill directories' — at the child's vantage). But the contract's *intent* was to audit current state, which was not communicated.

LESSON: when a spawn must reflect the parent's current working tree, either (a) do not use worktree isolation, (b) commit pending state before the spawn, or (c) pass data in via prompt rather than have the child re-read from disk. This is a real harness interaction the structural mock SP-001 could never have surfaced — first concrete value of running a real spawn.
